## prompt-3-mermaid-selection

*Generated by Process A from `Clinical-AI-Demos/.../ai-outputs/output-01/ChatGPT.md` and `output-01/README.md`, adapted for the single-prompt v4.0 build.*

From the Mermaid diagram families in
`Clinical-AI-Demos/tree/main/ai-outputs/output-01/ChatGPT.md` (the original
workflow flowchart, the PlantUML-inspired component and sequence diagrams, the
D2-inspired declarative map, the Excalidraw sketch board, the Graphviz/DOT
clustered flow, the Structurizr/C4 architecture view, the BPMN workflow, the
draw.io swimlane layout, and the hybrid diagram), produce a curated set of
**gray-scale** Mermaid diagrams for H. R. 9510 v4.0. The source diagrams are
colored; convert every one to gray-scale (use the `neutral` theme and explicit
gray `classDef` fills such as `#f5f5f5`, `#d9d9d9`, `#9e9e9e` with black strokes
and black text). Use different and most relevant diagram types throughout the
bill, so no two bill figures repeat the same Mermaid pattern unnecessarily.

Re-theme the diagram content from the software-publication workflow to the bill's
own subject: the four-work evidence-to-law lineage, the ten-gate verification
funnel and decision rule, the statutory layering through Title 21, the
pre-introduction path, the prompt and bill-version evolution, and the
build-and-handoff transparency process. For each diagram, give the gray-scale
Mermaid source and a one-line note on which bill figure slot and which document
genre it serves, so Stage 4 (figure selection) and the bill stages can place
them. State that each Markdown diagram is reproduced in the compiled LaTeX bill as
a matching gray-scale TikZ figure (no images, Rule 5).

Output `auto-bill-01/03-mermaid-selection/output-3-mermaid-selection.md` plus a
directory `README.md` naming the upstream files used. Commit each file separately
and push in real time within a single pull request. Use only Claude Code Opus 4.8
(1M context) Max. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
